name: sidecar-display
title: Sidecar Display
description: Connect your iPad over Sidecar and force it to extend instead of mirror — via BetterDisplay or a native helper, without touching your main display.
author: chiptoma

commands.name: connect-sidecar
commands.title: Connect Sidecar
commands.subtitle: Sidecar Display
commands.description: Connect the iPad and force the configured display mode.

commands.name: auto-reconnect
commands.title: Auto-Reconnect Sidecar
commands.subtitle: Sidecar Display
commands.description: Restore the Sidecar link after it drops (e.g. waking from sleep). Enable background refresh on this command to keep the iPad connected; run it manually to reconnect now.

commands.name: fix-mirroring
commands.title: Fix Mirroring
commands.subtitle: Sidecar Display
commands.description: Reconnect the main BetterDisplay virtual screen to clear macOS Sidecar's own mirror mode when the iPad connects showing a copy of your main screen. Briefly reshuffles the desktop. Needs BetterDisplay.

commands.name: sidecar-status
commands.title: Sidecar Status
commands.subtitle: Sidecar Display
commands.description: Menu-bar item showing the iPad name and connection status, with connect, disconnect, and extend/mirror actions. It does not auto-refresh (to stay friendly with menu-bar managers like Bartender); enable Background Refresh on this command if you want live status.

preferences.name: backend
preferences.title: Engine
preferences.description: Automatic uses BetterDisplay when its CLI is installed, otherwise the bundled Native helper. BetterDisplay drives betterdisplaycli (proven, needs the app). Native uses macOS SidecarCore and CoreGraphics with no dependency.

preferences.name: showDeviceName
preferences.title: Menu Bar
preferences.label: Show the connected device name next to the icon
preferences.description: Off keeps a constant-width icon that is friendlier to menu-bar managers like Bartender. On shows the device name when connected.

preferences.name: fixMirrorAfterConnect
preferences.title: Fix Mirroring
preferences.label: Fix mirroring on a fresh connect
preferences.description: Reconnects the main virtual screen only when the iPad newly attaches (not when re-running connect on an already-connected iPad), to clear macOS Sidecar's own mirror mode. That mirror mode is invisible to the display APIs, so this cannot detect it directly — it fires on every fresh connect, and briefly reshuffles the desktop. Requires BetterDisplay; ignored without it. Turn off to trigger it manually with the Fix Mirroring command.

preferences.name: displayMode
preferences.title: Display Mode
preferences.description: How the iPad should sit in the arrangement once connected.

preferences.name: ipadName
preferences.title: iPad Name
preferences.description: Leave empty to auto-detect. Required only if you have more than one Sidecar device.

preferences.name: fastReconnectAttempts
preferences.title: Fast Reconnect Attempts
preferences.description: Quick reconnect attempts after a drop before slowing to the heartbeat. Waking the Mac restarts the fast phase. Never gives up entirely.

preferences.name: backoffBaseSeconds
preferences.title: Backoff Base (seconds)
preferences.description: Initial wait between fast reconnect attempts; it doubles each try up to the cap. Note: the background command only runs about once a minute, so values under ~60s effectively mean 'every tick'.

preferences.name: backoffCapSeconds
preferences.title: Backoff Cap (seconds)
preferences.description: Longest wait the doubling backoff reaches during the fast phase.

preferences.name: slowRetrySeconds
preferences.title: Slow Retry (seconds)
preferences.description: How often to retry once the fast attempts are spent and the iPad is still absent.

preferences.name: wakeThresholdSeconds
preferences.title: Wake Threshold (seconds)
preferences.description: A gap this long between background ticks is treated as the Mac having slept, so the next tick reconnects immediately and restarts the fast phase.

preferences.name: settleTimeoutSeconds
preferences.title: Settle Timeout
preferences.description: Seconds to wait for a display change to take effect. Clamped to 2-60.

scripts.verify: npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test:unit
scripts.clean: rm -rf .test-build dist compiled_raycast_swift raycast-env.d.ts swift/.build swift/.swiftpm swift/.raycast-swift-build
scripts.store:check: npm run build && npm run test:unit
scripts.publish: npm run store:check && npm run preflight && npx @raycast/api@latest publish
scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

ERROR: This is a Raycast extension, not an npm package.
Run 'npm run publish' to submit it to the Raycast Store.

" && exit 1
